title: Runtime evidence weighting in FORGE
description: build_incident_response in surface_payloads.py assembles FORGE reasoning as: details['forge']['reasoning'] + replica_summary.get('runtime_comparison_summary'). The problem: best_mitigation_outcome_class and runtime_comparison_summary are both empty strings in scaffold/inferred mode because _runtime_override_mitigations only populates outcome_class when a real runtime_result (Docker exec) exists. Fix: in build_replica_summary, after building tested_mitigations via build_mitigation_checks, assign a deterministic outcome_class to each mitigation based on its confidence_delta and the issue_family. Then _runtime_comparison_summary will have something to work with and runtime_comparison_summary will be non-empty. Also: build_incident_response currently returns 'runbook' not 'forge' as the key — the reasoning lives at r['runbook']['reasoning']. That is fine and matches incident.js (line 312: setText('forgeReasoning', data.runbook.selection_logic || data.runbook.reasoning)). Do not rename it. Just make the reasoning string cite the outcome.
done_when: build_incident_response('INC001')['runbook']['reasoning'] contains 'resolved', 'improved', 'validated', 'runtime', or 'mitigation'
build_incident_response('INC002')['runbook']['reasoning'] contains same
build_incident_response('INC001')['replica_summary']['best_mitigation_outcome_class'] is non-empty string
build_incident_response('INC001')['replica_summary']['runtime_comparison_summary'] is non-empty string
test_gates: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.');  from server.services.surface_payloads import build_incident_response; r = build_incident_response('INC001'); rs = r['replica_summary']; assert rs.get('best_mitigation_outcome_class'), f'INC001 best_mitigation_outcome_class is empty: {rs}'; print('outcome_class OK:', rs['best_mitigation_outcome_class'])"

python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.');  from server.services.surface_payloads import build_incident_response; r = build_incident_response('INC001'); reasoning = r['runbook']['reasoning'].lower(); assert any(w in reasoning for w in ['resolved','improved','validated','runtime','mitigation']), f'INC001 FORGE reasoning does not cite outcome: {r[\"runbook\"][\"reasoning\"]}'; print('FORGE reasoning OK')"

python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.');  from server.services.surface_payloads import build_incident_response; r = build_incident_response('INC002'); rs = r['replica_summary']; assert rs.get('best_mitigation_outcome_class'), f'INC002 best_mitigation_outcome_class is empty'; print('INC002 outcome_class OK:', rs['best_mitigation_outcome_class'])"
files_likely_touched: server/services/enterprise_runtime.py — build_mitigation_checks: add outcome_class to each returned dict based on confidence_delta (>0.07=resolved, >0.03=improved, else=inferred_only); build_replica_summary: after build_mitigation_checks, ensure _runtime_comparison_summary falls back to a deterministic string from tested_mitigations when runtime_result is None

title: Runtime-aware GUARDIAN posture
description: The GUARDIAN posture logic is fully implemented in the live graph path (_guardian_node ~line 856). It already distinguishes resolved/improved/inferred evidence states and writes different validated_clause strings. The problem: build_incident_response in surface_payloads.py returns guardian reasoning directly from details['guardian']['reasoning'] in incident_payloads.py — which is the static fixture string ('GUARDIAN approved because...'). It never runs the live posture enrichment. Fix: in build_incident_response, after building replica_summary, enrich the guardian reasoning the same way _guardian_node does — prepend the validated_clause based on best_mitigation_outcome_class.
done_when: build_incident_response('INC001')['guardian']['reasoning'] contains 'reproduced', 'validated', 'inferred', 'runtime', 'resolved', or 'improved'
build_incident_response('INC002')['guardian']['reasoning'] contains same — different text from INC001 because INC002 resolves (pool clears) while INC001 improves (retry still present)
test_gates: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.');  from server.services.surface_payloads import build_incident_response; r = build_incident_response('INC001'); g = r['guardian']['reasoning'].lower(); assert any(w in g for w in ['reproduced','validated','inferred','runtime','resolved','improved']), f'INC001 guardian reasoning is generic: {r[\"guardian\"][\"reasoning\"]}'; print('GUARDIAN INC001 OK')"

python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.');  from server.services.surface_payloads import build_incident_response; r = build_incident_response('INC002'); g = r['guardian']['reasoning'].lower(); assert any(w in g for w in ['reproduced','validated','inferred','runtime','resolved','improved']), f'INC002 guardian reasoning is generic: {r[\"guardian\"][\"reasoning\"]}'; print('GUARDIAN INC002 OK')"
files_likely_touched: server/services/surface_payloads.py — in build_incident_response, after replica_summary is built, compute validated_clause from best_mitigation_outcome_class (same logic as _guardian_node ~line 904), prepend it to details['guardian']['reasoning'] before returning

title: TRACE developer handoff packet v2
description: build_trace_summary is correctly implemented — when reproduction_status == 'reproduced' it populates inspection_point, suspected_modules, suspected_functions, state_anomalies. The problem: replay_status_code and replay_duration_ms are None in scaffold/inferred mode (no Docker exec), so the condition 'if replay_status_code == 504 and replay_duration_ms' never fires and the state_anomalies list does not include the runtime replay evidence line. Fix: after item 9 sets best_mitigation_outcome_class and runtime_comparison_summary, those will be non-empty. build_trace_summary already reads runtime_comparison from replica_summary.get('runtime_comparison_summary') at line 1806 and appends it to state_anomalies when truthy. So fixing item 9 will cascade and fix item 11 automatically. Verify this is the case after item 9 lands. If inspection_point still does not cite runtime evidence, add a fallback sentence citing best_mitigation_action and best_mitigation_outcome_class from replica_summary.
done_when: build_incident_response('INC001')['trace_summary']['inspection_point'] length > 80 chars and is not the default placeholder
build_incident_response('INC001')['trace_summary']['state_anomalies'] is non-empty list
build_incident_response('INC002')['trace_summary']['suspected_modules'] is non-empty list
test_gates: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.');  from server.services.surface_payloads import build_incident_response; r = build_incident_response('INC001'); ts = r['trace_summary']; ip = ts.get('inspection_point',''); assert len(ip) > 80 and 'Wait for REPLICA' not in ip, f'INC001 TRACE inspection_point is placeholder or short: {ip}'; print('TRACE INC001 OK:', ip[:80])"

python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.');  from server.services.surface_payloads import build_incident_response; r = build_incident_response('INC002'); ts = r['trace_summary']; assert ts.get('suspected_modules'), 'INC002 TRACE must have suspected_modules'; print('TRACE INC002 modules OK:', ts['suspected_modules'])"
files_likely_touched: server/services/enterprise_runtime.py — build_trace_summary: if state_anomalies is still empty after the standard logic and runtime_comparison is empty, add a fallback anomaly line using replica_summary['best_mitigation_action'] and ['best_mitigation_outcome_class'] directly

title: Runtime comparison UI refinement
description: The frontend currently shows runtime comparison as a prose sentence in #replicaComparison (incident.html line 251, incident.js line 524). The operator cannot quickly compare baseline vs mitigated at a glance. Add a structured two-row block: baseline row (status, duration) vs best-mitigation row (status, duration, outcome label). Use these existing replica_summary fields: replay_status_code, replay_duration_ms, best_mitigation_status_code, best_mitigation_duration_ms, best_mitigation_outcome_class, best_mitigation_action. The four DOM IDs needed by the new Playwright tests are: #runtimeComparisonBlock, #runtimeBaselineRow, #runtimeMitigatedRow, #runtimeOutcomeLabel. These do not exist yet. Add them inside the existing REPLICA card in incident.html, below #replicaComparison. Populate them in incident.js after the replicaComparison setText call. For scaffold/inferred mode (no real status codes), show the outcome_class label from best_mitigation_outcome_class and the action from best_mitigation_action as the mitigated row. Frontend is vanilla JS — no React, no build step.
done_when: #runtimeComparisonBlock, #runtimeBaselineRow, #runtimeMitigatedRow, #runtimeOutcomeLabel exist in incident.html
Playwright test 'INC001 runtime comparison block' passes
Playwright test 'INC002 runtime comparison block' passes
npm run browser:verify passes all tests (6 original + 4 new = 10 total)
Docker rebuild passes and comparison block visible on :7860
files_likely_touched: frontend/incident.html — inside the REPLICA card (after #replicaComparison line 251), add:
frontend/static/incident.js — after line 524 (replicaComparison setText), populate the four new IDs from replica_summary fields

title: Memory linkage to runtime outcomes
description: enrich_memory_with_runtime is called in the live graph path (line 781, 972) but build_incident_response in surface_payloads.py does NOT call it and does NOT return memory_hits at all. Check: build_incident_response('INC001') has no 'memory_hits' key in its response dict. Fix: (1) add enrich_memory_with_runtime import to surface_payloads.py, (2) in build_incident_response, call it after building replica_summary, (3) return memory_hits in the response dict. After this, the Playwright test and smoke check for why_now_fit will work because enrich_memory_with_runtime already sets why_now_fit correctly when action overlap >= 0.2. Note: build_memory_pack is an async method on KnowledgeService — surface_payloads.py uses sync code. Use a deterministic sync fallback: read memory from incident_payloads details and enrich it.
done_when: 'memory_hits' key exists in build_incident_response('INC001') response
at least one runbook in memory_hits has a why_now_fit note
smoke check '[7] Memory enrichment check' passes
test_gates: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.');  from server.services.surface_payloads import build_incident_response; r = build_incident_response('INC001'); mh = r.get('memory_hits'); assert mh is not None, 'memory_hits missing from response'; runbooks = mh.get('runbooks',[]); assert runbooks, 'no runbooks in memory_hits'; assert any(rb.get('why_now_fit') for rb in runbooks), f'no why_now_fit in runbooks: {runbooks}'; print('Memory INC001 OK')"
files_likely_touched: server/services/surface_payloads.py — import enrich_memory_with_runtime from enterprise_runtime; in build_incident_response, build a memory_hits dict from details['similar_past_incidents'] and details['recommended_runbooks'] (both already in incident_payloads), enrich with enrich_memory_with_runtime(memory_hits, replica_summary=replica_summary), add to returned dict
server/incident_payloads.py — verify INC001 and INC002 have 'recommended_runbooks' field (they do — check line 52 and 153)

title: End-to-end demo closure for INC001 and INC002
description: Update demo.py to print all 6 stages (SENTINEL, PRISM, REPLICA, TRACE, FORGE, GUARDIAN) for both INC001 and INC002 using build_incident_response. Update docs/DEMO_WALKTHROUGH.md to cover the runtime evidence narrative. Update docs/DEMO_CHEAT_SHEET.md with an INC001 vs INC002 side-by-side. Extend local_enterprise_smoke.sh to verify memory_hits and the new forge/guardian runtime reasoning on the live Docker container.
done_when: python demo.py exits 0 and output covers REPLICA, TRACE, FORGE, GUARDIAN for both incidents
docs/DEMO_CHEAT_SHEET.md has INC001 vs INC002 side-by-side with outcome differences
All 10 Playwright tests pass
Docker smoke passes all 7 checks including memory enrichment

docs/DEMO_CHEAT_SHEET.md — add INC001 vs INC002 side-by-side table
